title: Implementation Architecture for Personal Portfolio Static Website
executive_summary: This architecture defines a static personal portfolio website built with HTML and CSS, hosted on Vercel. It targets employers, clients, collaborators, and personal contacts. The site includes About, Projects, Contact, Blog, Testimonials, and Résumé sections. The design emphasizes simplicity, accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA), performance optimization, and maintainability. No backend or data storage is required. Deployment and observability are handled through Vercel’s integrated CI/CD and analytics tools.
architecture_overview: A single-tier static architecture served via Vercel’s CDN edge network. The site consists of modular HTML pages styled with CSS and optionally enhanced with lightweight JavaScript. Content updates occur via Git commits. Optional integrations include Formspree for contact forms and Plausible or Vercel Analytics for metrics. The architecture ensures HTTPS-only access, global caching, and minimal operational overhead.

technology_stack.hosting: Vercel static hosting with CDN edge caching
technology_stack.version_control: GitHub or GitLab integrated with Vercel

functional_feature_map.About Me: Static section introducing the individual, skills, and background.
functional_feature_map.Projects: Grid or card layout showcasing selected projects with images and links.
functional_feature_map.Contact: Static mailto link or Formspree integration for inquiries.
functional_feature_map.Blog: Static posts updated manually via Git or Markdown files.
functional_feature_map.Testimonials: Curated quotes from clients or collaborators.
functional_feature_map.Résumé: Downloadable PDF résumé with preview section.

system_components.frontend_ui: Static HTML/CSS templates for each section.
system_components.styling_system: Global CSS with responsive grid/flexbox layout.
system_components.content_management: Manual Git-based updates or Markdown workflow for blog posts.
system_components.contact_integration: Static mailto link or Formspree form relay.
system_components.deployment_pipeline: Git-based CI/CD pipeline deploying to Vercel.
system_components.observability_layer: Vercel Analytics, uptime monitoring, and deployment logs.

workflows.content_update: Edit HTML or Markdown files locally, commit to Git, and push to main branch. Vercel automatically rebuilds and deploys.
workflows.deployment: Automatic deployment triggered by Git push to main branch. Preview deployments for pull requests.
workflows.contact_submission: Handled via mailto link or Formspree relay; no data stored locally.
workflows.blog_update: Manual addition of Markdown or HTML files for new posts.
workflows.rollback: Instant revert to previous deployment via Vercel dashboard.

data_model.storage_design: Static files hosted on Vercel CDN with public read-only access.
data_model.retention_policy: No data retention; all content managed via Git history.
data_model.analytics_data: Aggregated, anonymized metrics only if analytics enabled.

api_design.security: No client-side secrets; all API keys stored in Vercel environment variables if used.

controls: No backend or user data storage
Email obfuscation or third-party form relay
Least-privilege access for Git and Vercel accounts with 2FA
security_and_compliance.privacy: No personal data collected; privacy notice clarifies minimal data exposure.
security_and_compliance.incident_response: Restore from Git, verify DNS/SSL, redeploy via Vercel.

environments.development: Local workstation using VS Code and Git.
environments.production: Vercel production environment with HTTPS and CDN caching.

deployment_and_operations.backup: Git repository serves as primary backup; continuous via commits.

observability.logging: Vercel deployment logs and build output
observability.alerting: Email or dashboard alerts for downtime or deployment failures

cost_and_scaling.scaling_model: Automatic via Vercel CDN edge network

phased_implementation.phase_1: Deliver MVP static site with all sections using HTML/CSS on Vercel.
phased_implementation.phase_2: Add accessibility and performance optimizations (WCAG 2.1 AA, Lighthouse ≥90).
phased_implementation.phase_3: Integrate optional analytics and uptime monitoring.
phased_implementation.phase_4: Introduce Markdown-based blog workflow or lightweight CMS if needed.

risks_and_tradeoffs.simplicity_vs_scalability: Static architecture maximizes simplicity but limits dynamic content management.
risks_and_tradeoffs.manual_updates_vs_automation: Manual Git updates are simple but may become tedious for frequent blog posts.
risks_and_tradeoffs.privacy_vs_functionality: Avoiding backend enhances privacy but limits interactivity.
risks_and_tradeoffs.performance_vs_design_flexibility: Highly optimized assets may constrain design experimentation.

open_questions_resolved.Q1: Blog content updated manually via Git or Markdown workflow.
open_questions_resolved.Q2: Contact section uses Formspree integration or obfuscated mailto link.
open_questions_resolved.Q3: Accessibility target WCAG 2.1 AA; performance target Lighthouse ≥90.

thinking_summary: All requirements are confirmed for a low-risk static personal portfolio website built with HTML and CSS, hosted on Vercel. There are no unresolved focus issues. The architecture must remain simple, secure, and maintainable while addressing accessibility, performance, and content update workflows. The plan below consolidates all confirmed requirements and specialist recommendations into an implementation-grade architecture ready for execution.
